content:
# 3-Thread Pipeline for Direct Mode — Design Decision (2026-04-10)

## What
Refactor `raune_filter.py::run_single_process()` from single-threaded serial loop to
3-thread producer-consumer pipeline (decoder → GPU → encoder) with bounded queues.

## Why
- Current single-threaded mode forces serial execution of stages that run on different
  hardware units (CPU decoder, GPU, CPU encoder)
- Theoretical pipelined ceiling: ~12.5 fps; actual: 2.35 fps
- ~200-300ms/frame wasted on serialization

## How
- Python `threading.Thread` + `queue.Queue(maxsize=2)`
- PyAV releases GIL during decode/encode (libav calls)
- torch.cuda releases GIL during CUDA operations
- `None` sentinel for termination, shared errors list + stop_event for error handling
- Only Thread 2 touches CUDA — no thread-safety concerns

## Alternatives Rejected
- multiprocessing: IPC serialization cost, separate GPU contexts
- asyncio: decode/encode not natively async, more complex
- Rust-side parallelism: larger refactor, out of scope

## Issue
chunzhe10/dorea#64

## Spec
docs/decisions/2026-04-10-direct-mode-3thread-pipeline.md
